comment_text.sn7.18:4.2: The brahmin takes tidiva as the Brahmā realm rather than that of Indra (see note on SN 3.21:15.5).
comment_text.sn7.18:5.2: Here dhātu means loka (“realm”), not “element”.
comment_text.sn7.18:5.4: The Buddha rejects the brahmin’s supposition (see MN 26:15.35 and note).

root_text.sn7.18:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn7.18:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhāradvājagottassa brāhmaṇassa sambahulā antevāsikā kaṭṭhahārakā māṇavakā yena vanasaṇḍo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā addasaṁsu bhagavantaṁ tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā. Disvāna yena bhāradvājagotto brāhmaṇo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhāradvājagottaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn7.18:1.4: Asukasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe samaṇo nisinno pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā”.
root_text.sn7.18:1.5: Atha kho bhāradvājagotto brāhmaṇo tehi māṇavakehi saddhiṁ yena so vanasaṇḍo tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.sn7.18:1.6: Addasā kho bhagavantaṁ tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.sn7.18:1.7: Disvāna yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn7.18:3.1: Na yattha gītaṁ napi yattha vāditaṁ,
root_text.sn7.18:7.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhāradvājagotto brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:

translation_text.sn7.18:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn7.18:1.2: Then several students, pupils of one of the Bhāradvāja brahmins, approached a forest grove while collecting firewood. They saw the Buddha sitting down cross-legged at the root of a certain sal tree, his body set straight, and mindfulness established in his presence. Seeing this, they went up to Bhāradvāja and said to him,
translation_text.sn7.18:1.4: In such and such a forest grove the ascetic Gotama is sitting down cross-legged, his body set straight, and mindfulness established in his presence.”
translation_text.sn7.18:1.5: Then Bhāradvāja together with those young students went to that forest grove
translation_text.sn7.18:1.6: where he saw the Buddha sitting down cross-legged, his body set straight, and mindfulness established in his presence.
translation_text.sn7.18:1.7: He went up to the Buddha and addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn7.18:2.1: “Deep in the jungle so full of terrors,
translation_text.sn7.18:2.2: you’ve plunged into the empty, desolate wilderness.
translation_text.sn7.18:3.1: Where there is no song or music,
translation_text.sn7.18:3.2: a lonely sage resorts to the wilderness.
translation_text.sn7.18:3.3: This strikes me as an amazing thing,
translation_text.sn7.18:3.4: that you dwell so joyfully alone in the jungle.
translation_text.sn7.18:4.1: I suppose you wish to be reborn in the company
translation_text.sn7.18:4.2: of the supreme world-sovereign of the Third Heaven.
translation_text.sn7.18:4.3: Is that why you resort to the desolate wilderness,
translation_text.sn7.18:4.4: to practice fervor for attaining divinity?”
translation_text.sn7.18:5.1: “Any wishes and hopes that are always attached
translation_text.sn7.18:5.2: to the many and various realms—
translation_text.sn7.18:5.3: the yearnings sprung from the root of unknowing—
translation_text.sn7.18:5.4: I’ve eliminated them all down to the root.
translation_text.sn7.18:6.2: amongst all things, my vision is clear.
translation_text.sn7.18:6.3: I’ve attained the state of grace, <j>the supreme awakening;
translation_text.sn7.18:6.4: I meditate alone, brahmin, and self-assured.”
translation_text.sn7.18:7.1: When he had spoken, Bhāradvāja said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.18:7.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
